content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, a tennis game involves 444 referees.

Each referee has to point out whether he considers the ball to be inside limits or outside limits. The ball is considered to be `IN` if and only if **all** the referees agree that it was inside limits.

Given the decision of the 444 referees, help Chef determine whether the ball is considered inside limits or not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing 444 integers R1,R2,R3,R4R_1, R_2, R_3, R_4R1​,R2​,R3​,R4​ denoting the decision of the respective referees.

Here RRR can be either 000 or 111 where 000 would denote that the referee considered the ball to be inside limits whereas 111 denotes that they consider it to be outside limits.



Output Format
For each test case, output `IN` if the ball is considered to be inside limits by all referees and `OUT` otherwise.


The checker is case-insensitive so answers like `in`, `In`, and `IN` would be considered the same.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers R1 R2 R3 R4, where each Ri is either 0 (the i‑th referee thinks the ball is IN) or 1 (the i‑th referee thinks the ball is OUT).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "IN" if all four referees gave 0, otherwise output "OUT".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Not all referees gave 0, so OUT.
Test case 2: All four values are 0, so IN.
Test case 3: The last referee gave 1, so OUT.
Test case 4: All gave 1, so OUT.

hidden.label: edge case – single IN among OUTs

solution.approach: For each test case read the four decisions. The ball is considered IN only when every decision equals 0. Therefore check if R1==0 && R2==0 && R3==0 && R4==0. If true print "IN", else print "OUT". The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
